name: item
title: Item
description: A generic, composable media/content/action row primitive — the building block for settings rows, notification rows, search results, and list rows. ItemMedia (leading icon/avatar/thumbnail), ItemContent with ItemTitle/ItemDescription, and ItemActions (trailing controls) compose inside an Item, and ItemGroup lays out a list with spacing or hairline dividers. Independent of the Icon component, logical-property layout that mirrors under RTL, and default/outline/muted surfaces that stay visible in forced-colors mode.
useWhen: Building a settings row with a title, description, and a trailing control such as a Switch
Laying out a media row with a leading avatar or thumbnail plus trailing actions
Composing a grouped list of notification, search-result, or content rows with consistent spacing or dividers
avoidWhen: Presenting a self-contained summary with a heading, media, and footer (use a Card)
Rendering tabular records that users compare across columns (use a table)
Building a menu of commands with roving focus (use a dropdown-menu)
Making the whole row a click target while it also contains other separately focusable controls

keyboard: Item and its structural slots add no keyboard behavior or tab stops
Interactive descendants (links, buttons, a Switch) retain native keyboard behavior and visible focus indicators
When the whole row is one link or button, the padded row is already at least 44px so it meets the minimum pointer target
ItemActions is moved to the inline end with margin-inline-start:auto while DOM order stays media, content, actions
accessibility.targetSize: Inherited from interactive descendants; Item itself is not a control. An interactive whole-row is padded to at least 44px.
notes: The root adds no role or accessible name so it never competes with the semantics of the controls it wraps.
ItemTitle is a neutral element (not a heading) so a long list of rows does not inject headings into the page outline.
The outline and muted surfaces keep a visible border (transparent borders resolve to CanvasText) so the row boundary survives forced-colors mode; state is never conveyed by color alone.
ItemMedia adds no role or name — a child img keeps its own alt text and decorative images should use alt="".
Item does not depend on the Icon component; ItemMedia renders whatever leading visual the consumer provides.
